pick.rationale: UConn is a No. 6-ranked powerhouse from the Big East with a 29-4 record and a recent conference tournament run, showcasing their readiness for March. The Huskies possess far superior talent, size, and athleticism compared to Furman, whose efficiency ratings rank outside the top 190 nationally. While Furman is healthy and has improved its interior presence, the vast gulf in high-major experience and overall team quality makes a monumental 15-seed upset highly improbable.

reasoningStep.title: Evaluating the 2 vs 15 Seed Mismatch
reasoningStep.summary: UConn's elite national standing, high-major pedigree, and significant efficiency advantage over a mid-major Furman team create a mismatch with minimal upset risk.
reasoningStep.evidence: UConn is ranked #6 nationally with a 29-4 (17-3 Big East) record and just advanced to the Big East tournament final. Furman is a 15-seed from the Southern Conference with a 22-12 record, ranking 191st in KenPom and 195th in Torvik with sub-200 adjusted efficiency ratings on both offense and defense. No significant injuries were reported for either team. Historical context heavily favors the 2-seed in this matchup.

result.text: UConn 67-51 Georgetown (Mar 13, 2026) Game Recap - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search UConn Huskies vs Georgetown Hoyas Big East Tournament - Semifinal Georgetown Hoyas Georgetown GTWN 16-18 , 6-14 Big East 51 16-18 , 6-14 Big East Final 1 2 T Georgetown GTWN 21 30 51 UConn CONN 32 35 67 6 UConn Huskies UConn CONN 29-4 , 17-3 Big East 67 29-4 , 17-3 Big East Gamecast Recap Box Score Play-by-Play Team Stats No. 6 UConn beats Georgetown 67-51 to set up a Big East final clash against No. 13 St. John's By AP Mar 13, 2026, 10:50 pm Facebook Facebook Messenger Twitter Email NEW YORK -- — Braylon Mullins scored 15 of his 21 points in the first half, Jayden Ross hit a couple of momentum-stunting 3-pointers in the second, and sixth-ranked UConn defeated Georgetown 67-51 on Friday night to set up a showdown of the top two seeds in the Big East Tournament final. The second-seeded Huskies (29-4) will face regular-season conference champion and 13th-ranked St. John's in the title game Saturday night after the Red Storm beat Seton Hall 78-68 in the other semifinal. Like St. John's, UConn has yet to trail in either of its first two games this week at Madison Square Garden. Mullins scored five of the team's first seven points to go up 7-0, and the closest 11th-seeded Georgetown (16-18) got after that was down three at the 7 1/2-minute mark. UConn led by 11 at halftime, despite not attempting a free throw to that point, and finished with assists on 21 of its 28 field goals. Without leading scorer KJ Lewis , who was ruled out for the season with a left ankle injury, the Hoyas were ultimately overmatched by a stacked opponent they played tight in January and again in February . Although not as dominant as in a quarterfinal rout of Xavier, coach Dan Hurley's team has responded as he had hoped from what he called a “choke job” loss last weekend at Marquette that dropped UConn two spots in the AP Top 25 . That defeat and the “brutal film session” that Hurley said followed might turn out to be an important learning moment for the Huskies — if they keep this level of play going and make another lengthy NCAA Tournament run two years since the program won the second of back-to-back national championships. Up next A rubber match for the conference's top two teams after they split the two-game season series . UConn won the second meeting on Feb. 25 by 32 points. ------ Get poll alerts and updates on the AP Top 25 throughout the season. Sign up here and here (AP mobile app). The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament predictions and preview for UConn vs. Furman. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Predictions & Preview: UConn vs. Furman Here are the odds for this opening-round matchup of the 2026 NCAA Tournament. Let’s dive into our preview and predictions for this NCAA Tournament matchup. UConn 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Dan Hurley’s team regrouped well after its second-round exit last March. The Huskies went 27-4 in the regular season and reclaimed the Big East summit with a 17-3 conference record. Their 72-40 dismantling of St. John’s was the first time UConn held a Big East opponent to 20% shooting or worse in more than 30 seasons, and it said everything about UConn's ceiling. Alex Karaban is the senior heartbeat of this team. He moved into the top 10 in program history in career scoring during the regular-season finale and scored 23 points on senior day to will his team to a comeback win over Seton Hall. Tarris Reed Jr. is as dominant a rebounder as there is in college basketball, anchoring an interior defense that ranks top 10 nationally in opponent two-point shooting and block percentage. Solo Ball has done well in his second full season as a starter, averaging 13.5 points per game, and freshman Braylon Mullins has given UConn a perimeter weapon to go with Silas Demary Jr., who owns a top-15 assist rate nationally. The Huskies rank top 10 in assists per game and assist-to-field goal percentage. Nothing goes to waste. There are holes to poke, as losses to Creighton and Marquette were confusing red flags for the tournament. The Huskies do a lot of things really well, but it's hard to say they do anything the best. Basket Under Review released a “Kemba Factor” ranking for potential takeover players in March, and UConn’s highest-rated player was Demary at No. 43. result.text: Furman March Madness bracket predictions for 2026 NCAA tournament Skip to main content Home Sports High Schools Clemson USC Furman Paladins Greenville Drive Swamp Rabbits Fantasy Sports SAIL Swimming USA TODAY Sports Where Furman basketball stands in March Madness bracket predictions on Selection Sunday Zachary Huber Hendersonville Times-News Show Caption Hide Caption Watch coach Bob Richey react to Furman basketball's March Madness bid Furman men's basketball coach Bob Richey speaks at the team's Selection Sunday watch party. "We'll see you at Philly!" The Furman Paladins men's basketball team has secured a spot in the NCAA Tournament. Bracketology experts project Furman will likely be a No. 15 seed in the tournament. Furman earned its bid by winning the Southern Conference tournament championship against East Tennessee State. Selection Sunday is finally here. The Furman Paladins men's basketball team earned its spot in the NCAA Tournament after winning the Southern Conference tournament championship on March 9. Furman (22-12) will learn its first-round opponent at 6 p.m. ET on March 15 on CBS or Paramount+ . The bracket reveal show can be streamed on Fubo , which carries CBS and offers a free trial to new subscribers. FUBO FREE TRIAL: Watch March Madness games on Fubo Furman will play in March Madness for the first time since 2023 after it defeated East Tennessee State 76-61 in the SoCon tournament final. Paladins forward Cooper Bowser turned in a double-double against the Buccanneers with 21 points and 11 rebounds. Bowser's performance was critical, as leading scorer Alex Wilkins had an off night shooting the ball, scoring only 12 points on 4-of-12 shooting from the field and 2-for-8 from the 3-point line. SLING TV: Watch Furman vs. UConn on Sling TV Furman learns its seed Furman earned a No. 15 seed and will face No. 2 UConn in Philadelphia on March 20 at 10 p.m. on TBS . The Paladins were placed in the East Region. Furman men's basketball March Madness projections Most bracketology updates predict that the Paladins will be a No. 15 seed in March Madness. ESPN's Joe Lunardi has Furman as the No. 15 seed in the East Region, going against No. 2 Iowa State in St. Louis. CBS has the Paladins as a No. 15 seed facing Houston. The last time Furman reached March Madness, it pulled one of the biggest upsets of the tournament. JP Pegues made a last-second 3-pointer for No. 13 Furman to knock off No. 4 Virginia. Zachary Huber is a high school sports reporter for the Hendersonville Times-News. Email him at zhuber@gannett.com or follow him on X @zacharyahuber. "We'll see you at Philly!" The Furman Paladins men's basketball team has secured a spot in the NCAA Tournament. Bracketology experts project Furman will likely be a No. 15 seed in the tournament. Furman earned its bid by winning the Southern Conference tournament championship against East Tennessee State. Selection Sunday is finally here. The Furman Paladins men's basketball team earned its spot in the NCAA Tournament after winning the Southern Conference tournament championship on March 9. Furman (22-12) will learn its first-round opponent at 6 p.m. ET on March 15 on CBS or Paramount+ . The bracket reveal show can be streamed on Fubo , which carries CBS and offers a free trial to new subscribers. FUBO FREE TRIAL: Watch March Madness games on Fubo Furman will play in March Madness for the first time since 2023 after it defeated East Tennessee State 76-61 in the SoCon tournament final. Paladins forward Cooper Bowser turned in a double-double against the Buccanneers with 21 points and 11 rebounds. Bowser's performance was critical, as leading scorer Alex Wilkins had an off night shooting the ball, scoring only 12 points on 4-of-12 shooting from the field and 2-for-8 from the 3-point line. SLING TV: Watch Furman vs. UConn on Sling TV Furman learns its seed Furman earned a No. 15 seed and will face No. 2 UConn in Philadelphia on March 20 at 10 p.m. on TBS . The Paladins were placed in the East Region. Furman men's basketball March Madness projections Most bracketology updates predict that the Paladins will be a No. 15 seed in March Madness. ESPN's Joe Lunardi has Furman as the No. 15 seed in the East Region, going against No. 2 Iowa State in St. Louis. CBS has the Paladins as a No. 15 seed facing Houston. The last time Furman reached March Madness, it pulled one of the biggest upsets of the tournament. JP Pegues made a last-second 3-pointer for No. 13 Furman to knock off No. 4 Virginia. Zachary Huber is a high school sports reporter for the Hendersonville Times-News. Email him at zhuber@gannett.com or follow him on X @zacharyahuber.
